primary_decisions_supported: Targeting and qualification criteria for sales
executive_questions_answered: Do deals that fit our Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) close at higher rates or generate more value than non-ICP deals?
What characteristics do our won deals consistently share (win profile)?
Should we adjust our ICP definition based on conversion and retention data?

metric_definitions.definition: The percentage of opportunities that are closed-won for those deals meeting Ideal Customer Profile criteria, compared to non-ICP deals.
metric_definitions.formula: ICP Win% = (# of ICP opportunities won / total ICP opportunities) * 100

known_variants.definition_delta: Could also be measured from lead to closed-won (pipeline conversion) for ICP leads vs others
known_variants.when_to_use: If analyzing at earlier funnel stages including marketing leads

pitfalls: Requires a clear, consistent ICP definition applied to each deal (e.g., flag in CRM)
Sample sizes: if ICP is narrow, win rate differences may not be statistically significant without enough data
Correlation vs causation: higher ICP win rate could be because sales focuses more on those deals; still a useful indicator

metric_definitions.definition: Common characteristics among won deals (e.g., company size, use-case, champion persona). Not a single metric but a set of profile attributes with above-average win rates.
metric_definitions.formula: n/a (analytical output: top attributes frequency in wins vs losses)
pitfalls: This is more qualitative/analytical – avoid drawing false conclusions (some traits might correlate with wins accidentally)
Ensure data on deal attributes (industry, persona, etc.) is well-captured in CRM; missing data can mislead patterns
Traits can evolve as product or market changes – today's win profile might shift tomorrow

required_fields.description: Yes/no if opp meets ICP criteria
Industry, Employee Count, Use Case, Competitor, etc.

joins_and_keys.notes: Bring account attributes (industry, size) to opp
Map persona info (role/title) of champion to opportunity

transformation_rules: Define ICP criteria clearly (e.g., specific industries, company size range, use-case fit) and ensure each opp is tagged ICP or not
Group attributes into meaningful buckets (e.g., industries by vertical, employee count into tiers) for analysis
Calculate win rates per attribute: e.g., win rate in Manufacturing industry vs Tech

data_quality_checks.logic: Every opportunity above a certain stage has ICP field filled (no null)
Ensure ICP was defined prior to outcome (to prevent only successful deals being tagged ICP retrospectively)

derived_fields: Win_Flag (1 for won, 0 for lost per opp)
ICP_Flag (1 if opp meets ICP, 0 if not)
aggregation_rules: Calculate win rate = avg(Win_Flag) for ICP=1 vs ICP=0 groups
Count wins by attribute (e.g., industry) and divide by total opps of that attribute to get attribute win rates
calculations_and_models.cohort_logic: Optionally analyze win rates by cohort (e.g., by quarter the opp was created) to ensure trends hold over time

layout.purpose: Compare close rates for opportunities inside vs outside ideal profile
Top attributes of won deals (e.g., break down win rates by industry or size)

narrative_insights_to_generate: Highlight how much higher the win rate is for ICP deals and what that implies
Describe common traits among won deals (e.g., 'our wins are mostly in X industry with Y size')
diagnostic_questions: Are there segments outside our defined ICP that actually have good win rates, indicating a potential new ICP expansion?
Which customer attributes correlate with faster sales cycles or larger deal sizes in wins (part of win profile)?

recommended_actions.action: Refine targeting criteria to focus on high-win segments
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: ICP win rate significantly exceeds non-ICP, indicating strong payoff in focusing on ICP
Some non-ICP segments show unexpectedly high win rates (consider including them as ICP or creating separate focus)

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Static analysis of win rates by a couple of key dimensions (industry, size) to evaluate ICP assumptions
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Dashboard with filters for any attribute and ML-derived 'win likelihood' scoring highlighting top profile combinations
common_client_customizations: Include retention or NRR of ICP vs non-ICP customers to see if ICP not only easier to close but also retain
Visualize win rates across lead sources for ICP leads (blending with marketing data)
privacy_and_access_notes: Deal attributes and ICP flags are internal – safe for internal sharing
Be careful in external comms: ICP definition and performance might be competitively sensitive
success_criteria: Sales team adopts ICP-based qualification with confidence (because data shows it matters)
Resource allocation (marketing budget, SDR focus) aligns to the identified high-win-profile segments

icp_win_rate_lift.significant: ICP win rate 1.5x+ higher than non-ICP
icp_win_rate_lift.not_significant: ICP win rate <1.2x higher (re-evaluate ICP definition)
